sections.paragraph:
ἀνεμπόδιστος, ον (s. ἐμποδίζω; Aristot., EN 7, 12 [1153a, 15]; 13 [1153b, 10f] et al.; Epict. 3, 22, 41; 4, 4, 5; Vett. Val. 246, 5; SIG2 517, 32 [SIG 955, 32 restores this passage difft.]; OGI 383, 129; pap since II b.c.], e.g. UPZ 191, 13; 192, 23; 193, 23; PAmh 38, 12; Wsd 17:19; 19:7; Just., D. 3, 2) unhindered only adv. in our lit ἀνεμποδίστως (Diod S 1, 36, 10; PTebt 6, 48; 43, 40 [118 b.c.] al. in pap; Jos., Ant. 16, 172) κλῆρον ἀ. ἀπολαβεῖν receive my lot unhindered IRo 1:2.—DELG s.v. πούς.

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

difft. difft. = different(ly)

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

PAmh PAmh = The Amherst Papyri I–II—List 4

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

IRo IRo = Ignatius to the Romans—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 77
